parameters.jsCode:
const imgData = (($input.first().json) || {}).images || [];
if (imgData.length === 0) return [{ json: { ollamaBody: null, hasImages: false } }];
const prompt = 'You are reading pages from UK company accounts filed at Companies House. Look carefully at every page and find these figures.

'
  + 'In UK accounts, tables usually show TWO columns: read the year in each column heading and take the column with the later date.

'
  + 'If the accounts show BOTH group/consolidated and individual company figures (for example a "Group" and a "Company" balance sheet, or consolidated and company columns), use the GROUP / CONSOLIDATED figures.

'
  + '1. Annual turnover or revenue (GBP) - current year
'
  + '2. Average number of employees - single total figure
'
  + '3. Net assets or total equity (GBP) - current year
'
  + '4. Profit BEFORE tax (GBP) - current year, negative if a loss
'
  + '5. Profit AFTER tax (GBP) - current year, negative if a loss
'
  + '6. Financial year end date

'
  + 'IMPORTANT: If column headings show figures are in thousands (e.g. £000 or £ 000), multiply each figure by 1000 to give the full amount. In UK accounts, figures shown in parentheses e.g. (£45,000) are negative. Always output negative values with a minus sign, not parentheses.

'
  + 'State each figure on its own line in exactly this format:
Turnover: £2,450,000
Employees: 45
Net assets: £890,000
Profit before tax: £150,000
Profit after tax: £123,456
Year: 2023

'
  + 'If a figure is a loss, write it with a minus sign e.g. Profit after tax: -£45,000
If you cannot find a figure, write null for that line. Do not list multiple values for the same field.';
const TOKENS_PER_PAGE = 1400; // measured for the converter's A4 page output; scales with pixel area
const CTX_HEADROOM = 6500; // prompt + response margin (matches tester so bucketed num_ctx values are identical)
const CTX_CAP = 65536; // ~40 GB total footprint on the 32b model - fits Amelai's GPUs
const CTX_STEP = 8192; // bucketed so similar-sized requests reuse the loaded runner
const maxPages = Math.floor((CTX_CAP - CTX_HEADROOM) / TOKENS_PER_PAGE);
if (imgData.length > maxPages) return [{ json: { ollamaBody: null, hasImages: false, debug: 'filing has ' + imgData.length + ' pages; vision cap is ' + maxPages } }];
const numCtx = Math.min(CTX_CAP, Math.max(8192, Math.ceil((imgData.length * TOKENS_PER_PAGE + CTX_HEADROOM) / CTX_STEP) * CTX_STEP));
const body = JSON.stringify({ model: 'qwen3-vl:32b', prompt: prompt, images: imgData, stream: false, options: { num_ctx: numCtx } });
return [{ json: { ollamaBody: body, hasImages: true } }];

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ message: { subject: $json.subject, body: { contentType: 'HTML', content: $json.htmlBody }, toRecipients: [{ emailAddress: { address: 'steve@portland-fuel.co.uk' } }], attachments: [{ '@odata.type': '#microsoft.graph.fileAttachment', name: 'Customer_Profiles_' + new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0] + '.csv', contentType: 'text/csv', contentBytes: $json.csvBase64 }] }, saveToSentItems: false }) }}

parameters.initialMessages:
Hi there welcome to the Portland Company Profiler.

Add one or more companies (one per line):
add 06880902 10 Hedging, Bulk | large arable farm, consistent volumes
add 12345678 8 HVO, Cards | regional haulier, growing fleet

The note after '|' is optional but recommended for lead scoring.
Without a note:
add 06880902 8 Bulk

Update fields on an existing company without re-searching financials:
update 06880902 Products=Hedging, Fuelcards | Ranking=10
(Products/SIC Codes are added to the existing list, not replaced. Other fields are overwritten. Field names must match a CSV column: Company Name, Region, Ranking, Products, SIC Codes, Turnover, Employees, Net Assets, Accounts Year, Confidence, Ranking Note, Profile Date)

Other commands (single line only):
remove 06880902
list

(Using 'add' on an existing company number will overwrite the existing entry)

options.subtitle: Enter one or more add commands (one per line): add <RegNo> <Ranking> <Products>. Or: update <RegNo> <Field>=<Value> | <Field>=<Value> / list / remove <RegNo>
options.title: Portland Company Profiler Entry
